editableNewDialogue.text: I’m checking a translation: does 「百の大典」 sound more like a book title or a ceremonial phrase?

It can read like either, but it *most naturally* feels like a lofty title (something like “The Grand Compendium of One Hundred” / “The Great Canon of a Hundred”). As a ceremonial phrase, it’s a bit unusual without context—if it’s from a fantasy setting, a “grand compendium/codex” vibe fits well.
If you had to pick one French rendering that sounds like an actual title, what would you choose?

« Le Grand Compendium des Cent » (or, slightly smoother: « Le Grand Compendium des Cent Recueils » if the “hundred” refers to volumes/collections). If you want something more literary: « Le Grand Codex des Cent ».
Also, is “compendium” used in French titles much?

Less common than « encyclopédie », « recueil », « somme », or « codex », but it does appear—often for academic or intentionally “pseudo-scholarly” titles. For a fantasy artifact-name, « codex » or « grimoire » usually sounds more idiomatic.
Unrelated: why do people call those Facebook prompts “chain posts”?

Because they propagate by asking each participant to pass it along (tag others / repost), forming a “chain” of shares—similar to chain letters. As a random example not tied to anyone in particular: a local library once did a “list 5 books” chain to boost lending stats, and it worked because it gamified participation.
Au fait, si jamais je reviens avec d’autres extraits comme ça, n’hésite pas à me les rendre en français : c’est surtout pour réviser avant mon contrôle.
Bien sûr—envoie-moi l’extrait quand tu veux, et je te le traduirai en français. Si tu me dis aussi le niveau/les points à travailler pour ton contrôle, je peux adapter le vocabulaire et la tournure.
Wait—small correction. In that Facebook “five favorite books” thing, I didn’t pick Kurimoto Kaoru’s グイン・サーガ. The long series I’ve kept reading is 田中芳樹の『銀河英雄伝説』のほう。
Ah, got it—so it wasn’t Kurimoto Kaoru’s Guin Saga. You mean the one you’ve been reading for a long time is Yoshiki Tanaka’s “Legend of the Galactic Heroes” (銀河英雄伝説).
Yes. グイン・サーガって書いたみたいになってたけど、それは違う。ずっと読み続けてるのは『銀英伝』だよ。

annotation.queryText: I’m trying to pick a single long-form series to stick with as my “default” reading when I don’t know what else to start. Based on what you know about the one saga I’ve consistently prioritized over other books for years, which series should that be—and why would choosing it fit my reading habits better than hopping between random standalone novels?
